annotations.label: Functional problem (general)
annotations.note: This is the standard Hahn–Banach extension formalized as `exists_extension_of_le_sublinear`.

annotations.label: Vector problem

anchor.snippet: of bounded linear functionals on a normed space

annotations.note: Historical naming of a problem statement, not a Mathlib definition.

annotations.label: Functional problem

anchor.snippet: of vectors in a normed space

annotations.note: Historical naming of a problem statement, not a Mathlib definition.

annotations.label: Dominated (above) by a function

anchor.snippet: is said to be dominated (above) by a function

annotations.note: Used inline as `∀ x, f x ≤ p x` throughout Mathlib but no dedicated `Dominated` predicate exists.

annotations.label: Characterization of convex functions with f(0) = 0

anchor.snippet: and all non-negative real

annotations.note: No characterization of convex functions vanishing at 0 via the sublinearity-style inequality is recorded in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Every sublinear function is convex

anchor.snippet: Every sublinear function is a convex function

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks an `IsSublinear` predicate but proves `Seminorm.convexOn`, the seminorm special case of the same fact.

annotations.label: Sublinear is seminorm iff balanced

anchor.snippet: A sublinear function is a seminorm if and only if it is a balanced function

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general sublinear-function class so this iff is not stated.

annotations.label: Real sublinear is seminorm iff symmetric

anchor.snippet: On a real vector space (although not on a complex vector space)

annotations.note: Same — without a sublinear-function class this real-case characterization is absent.

annotations.label: Identity function as sublinear non-seminorm

anchor.snippet: is an example of a sublinear function that is not a seminorm

annotations.note: No such illustrative example is recorded in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hahn–Banach dominated extension theorem (real)
annotations.note: The real sublinear-dominated extension is exactly `exists_extension_of_le_sublinear`.

annotations.label: Dominated complex-valued functional

anchor.section: For complex or real vector spaces
anchor.snippet: A complex-valued functional

annotations.note: No dedicated definition of complex-dominated functional; used inline as `∀ x, ‖f x‖ ≤ p x`.

annotations.label: Hahn–Banach dominated extension (succinct form)

anchor.section: For complex or real vector spaces
anchor.snippet: every dominated linear functional defined on a vector subspace

annotations.note: Stated for `RCLike` fields as `Module.Dual.exists_extension_of_le_seminorm`.

annotations.label: Linear functional determined by its real part

anchor.section: For complex or real vector spaces
anchor.snippet: is completely determined by its real part

annotations.note: Encoded as the linear equivalence `Module.Dual.extendRCLikeₗ : Dual ℝ F ≃ₗ[ℝ] Dual 𝕜 F`.

annotations.label: Linear functional dominated by seminorm iff real part is

anchor.section: For complex or real vector spaces
anchor.snippet: a linear functional is dominated by a seminorm

annotations.note: Mathlib has the (⇒) direction `Module.Dual.norm_extendRCLike_le_seminorm` from a real-part bound to a full-norm bound, not an explicit iff.

annotations.label: Continuity equivalent to continuity of real part

anchor.section: For complex or real vector spaces
anchor.snippet: is continuous if and only if this is true of its real part

annotations.note: Captured implicitly by `StrongDual.extendRCLikeₗ`, a linear equivalence between continuous real-linear and 𝕜-linear duals; no standalone iff lemma.

annotations.label: Hahn–Banach theorem (seminorm version)
annotations.note: Exactly `Module.Dual.exists_extension_of_le_seminorm` for fields satisfying `IsRCLikeNormedField`.

annotations.label: One-dimensional dominated extension lemma
annotations.note: The one-dimensional step is inlined inside the Zorn argument of `RieszExtension.step`/`exists_extension_of_le_sublinear` and not exposed as a separate lemma.

annotations.label: Norm-preserving linear extension

anchor.snippet: is said to be norm-preserving

annotations.note: No standalone definition; the property `‖g‖ = ‖f‖` is used inline in `exists_extension_norm_eq`.

annotations.label: Field is injective in locally convex category

anchor.snippet: is an injective object in the category of locally convex vector spaces

annotations.note: The category of locally convex spaces and its injective objects are not set up in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hahn–Banach continuous extension theorem
annotations.note: Exactly `StrongDual.exists_extension` for polynormable spaces over `IsRCLikeNormedField`.

annotations.label: Norm-preserving Hahn–Banach continuous extension theorem
annotations.note: `exists_extension_norm_eq` provides the norm-preserving extension on normed spaces over `RCLike`-like fields.

annotations.label: Norm-preserving iff dominated by seminorm of |f|

anchor.section: Proof of the continuous extension theorem
anchor.snippet: is norm-preserving if and only if the extension is dominated by the seminorm

annotations.note: This intermediate equivalence is not isolated as a Mathlib lemma; the proof of `exists_extension_norm_eq` uses the (⇐) direction directly.

annotations.label: Continuous extension characterization on non-locally-convex TVS

anchor.snippet: if and only if there exists some continuous seminorm

annotations.note: Mathlib has the existence direction via a continuous seminorm bound but not the iff for general (non-locally-convex) TVS.

annotations.label: Separation of convex sets (locally convex TVS)
annotations.note: Strict separation in a locally convex space is `geometric_hahn_banach_compact_closed` (compact/closed) with companion `geometric_hahn_banach_open` variants.

annotations.label: Hahn–Banach separation theorem (open / compact-closed)
annotations.note: Mathlib has both `geometric_hahn_banach_open` and `geometric_hahn_banach_compact_closed` covering the two cases.

annotations.label: Mazur's theorem
annotations.note: Mazur's theorem on weak closure of convex sets does not appear in Mathlib (only Gelfand–Mazur and Mazur–Ulam exist).

annotations.label: Separation of a subspace and an open convex set

anchor.value: Corollary [ 24 ] (Separation of a subspace and an open convex set)

annotations.note: No dedicated subspace/open-convex separation corollary is stated, though it would follow from `geometric_hahn_banach_open`.

annotations.label: Functionals detect the boundary of a convex set

anchor.snippet: geometric Hahn–Banach implies that functionals can detect the boundary of a set

annotations.note: No supporting-hyperplane-style boundary detection lemma found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Smooth normed space

anchor.snippet: Call a normed space

annotations.note: The notion of a (point-) smooth normed space (unique supporting functional) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Köthe: smoothness iff Gateaux differentiability of norm

anchor.snippet: Köthe showed in 1983 that a normed space is smooth at a point

annotations.note: Köthe's smoothness/Gateaux-differentiability characterization is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Separation by continuous linear functional from balanced neighborhood

anchor.snippet: be a convex balanced neighborhood of the origin in a locally convex topological vector space

annotations.note: Mathlib's `separate_convex_open_set` separates a point from a convex open neighborhood of 0, generalizing the balanced-neighborhood case.

annotations.label: Linear subspaces characterized by continuous functionals

anchor.snippet: linear subspaces are characterized by functionals

annotations.note: No standalone statement that closed subspaces equal the intersection of zero-sets of continuous functionals appears in Mathlib (only `iInter_halfSpaces_eq` for closed convex sets).

annotations.label: Natural injection into double dual is isometric

anchor.snippet: the natural injection

annotations.note: `NormedSpace.inclusionInDoubleDualLi` packages the canonical map into the bidual as a linear isometry.

annotations.label: Characterization of reflexive Banach spaces via separation
annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of reflexive Banach space, so this characterization is not formalized.

annotations.label: Closed TVS-isomorphic-to-K^I subspace is complemented
annotations.note: Only the finite-dimensional special case (`K^n`) is in Mathlib via `Submodule.ClosedComplemented.of_finiteDimensional`.

annotations.label: Extension via absorbing disk (template)
annotations.note: No general absorbing-disk template extension theorem is recorded in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hahn–Banach theorem for seminorms
annotations.note: `Module.Dual.exists_extension_of_le_seminorm` is the seminorm-bounded extension.

annotations.label: Hahn–Banach sandwich theorem
annotations.note: The sandwich form (concave ≤ linear ≤ sublinear) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Andenaes maximal dominated extension theorem
annotations.note: Andenaes' maximal extension theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vector-valued Hahn–Banach theorem
annotations.note: Only the finite-dimensional-codomain special case is in Mathlib via `ContinuousLinearMap.exist_extension_of_finiteDimensional_range`.

annotations.label: Commutative set of maps and G-invariant function

anchor.snippet: is commutative (with respect to function composition

annotations.note: No `G`-invariant sublinear/functional definition tied to an invariant Hahn–Banach setup is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Invariant Hahn–Banach theorem
annotations.note: The invariant Hahn–Banach theorem (under a commuting family) is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Mazur–Orlicz theorem
annotations.note: Mazur–Orlicz is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Extension principle for scalar functions
annotations.note: This generic scalar extension principle is not recorded in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Extension property and HBEP

anchor.snippet: has the extension property if any continuous linear functional

annotations.note: The Hahn–Banach extension property as a named definition is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kalton's converse to Hahn–Banach

anchor.snippet: every complete metrizable TVS with the Hahn–Banach extension property is locally convex

annotations.note: Kalton's converse is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Separation property of a subspace

anchor.snippet: has the separation property if for every element of X

annotations.note: No subspace-level separation property definition is in Mathlib.

annotations.note: The Boolean-algebra probability charge equivalence to Hahn–Banach is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hahn–Banach implies a non-Lebesgue measurable set exists

anchor.section: Relation to axiom of choice and other theorems
anchor.snippet: the Hahn–Banach theorem suffices to derive the existence of a non-Lebesgue measurable set

annotations.note: This metamathematical implication is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hahn–Banach implies Banach–Tarski paradox

anchor.section: Relation to axiom of choice and other theorems
anchor.snippet: the Hahn–Banach theorem implies the Banach–Tarski paradox

annotations.note: The Banach–Tarski paradox itself is not in Mathlib, hence neither is this implication.
